agent_output:
{
  "domain_analysis": {
    "business_context": "New 'Express Delivery' product offering faster delivery (target: 2 hours) at a premium fee, with a refund policy for missed delivery windows. Synthesized from raw product discovery workshop notes involving PM, Operations, Finance, Customer Success, and Legal stakeholders.",
    "bounded_context": "Express Delivery Fulfillment — covers order eligibility, dispatch, driver assignment, real-time tracking, SLA monitoring, and refund processing for premium express orders.",
    "ubiquitous_language": [
      { "term": "Express Delivery", "definition": "A premium delivery option targeting a 2-hour delivery window from order placement, available to eligible customers at an additional fee." },
      { "term": "Express Fee", "definition": "The premium surcharge ($12.99) charged on top of the base delivery fee for express delivery service." },
      { "term": "Base Delivery Fee", "definition": "The standard delivery charge applied to all orders, independent of delivery speed tier." },
      { "term": "Delivery Window", "definition": "The target elapsed time (2 hours) from order placement within which the express delivery should be completed." },
      { "term": "Pickup SLA", "definition": "The maximum time (15 minutes) allowed between order placement and physical pickup by a driver before the delivery window is considered at risk." },
      { "term": "Eligible Warehouse", "definition": "A warehouse within 10km of the customer's delivery address, used to determine express delivery availability." },
      { "term": "Express-Qualified Driver", "definition": "A driver with a minimum 4-star rating who is eligible to fulfill express deliveries." },
      { "term": "Driver Cancellation", "definition": "When an assigned driver abandons an in-progress express delivery, triggering reassignment protocols." },
      { "term": "Reassignment Window", "definition": "The 10-minute maximum allowed to assign a replacement driver after a driver cancellation before an automatic refund is triggered." },
      { "term": "Goodwill Refund", "definition": "A non-contractual refund of the express fee issued when the delivery window is missed, framed as a business courtesy rather than a legal obligation." },
      { "term": "Wrong Address Exception", "definition": "A refund exclusion condition where the customer provided an incorrect delivery address, voiding the delivery window commitment." }
    ]
  },

  "business_rules": [
    {
      "id": "BR-1",
      "rule": "Express delivery option is only available to customers whose delivery address is within 10km of at least one warehouse.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "Physical constraint — offering express outside the service radius would make the delivery window unachievable and corrupt the product promise. The system must never allow an express order to be placed outside this radius.",
      "testable_assertion": "Given a customer address more than 10km from all warehouses, the express delivery option must not be selectable."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-2",
      "rule": "The express fee is $12.99, charged in addition to the base delivery fee.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "Pricing is a business policy that can be changed by commercial teams. The invariant is that an express fee exists and is additive to the base fee; the exact amount is configurable.",
      "testable_assertion": "When a customer selects express delivery, the order total includes both the base delivery fee and the express fee of $12.99."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-3",
      "rule": "An express order must be picked up by a driver within 15 minutes of order placement.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "Operational threshold that may be tuned based on performance data. However, Operations considers this critical to meeting the 2-hour window.",
      "testable_assertion": "If no driver has picked up the order within 15 minutes of placement, the system flags the order as 'pickup SLA breached'."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-4",
      "rule": "Only drivers with a rating of 4 stars or above may be assigned to express deliveries.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "Quality gate protecting the express product's reliability. Assigning a sub-4-star driver to express would violate the operational contract and degrade service quality in a way that corrupts the express tier's meaning.",
      "testable_assertion": "Given a driver with a rating below 4.0, the system must never assign them to an express delivery order."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-5",
      "rule": "When the delivery window is missed, the express fee is refunded but the base delivery fee is not.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "Core refund scoping rule — refunding the base fee would mean the customer received free delivery entirely, which changes the commercial model. The express fee refund is the defined remediation scope.",
      "testable_assertion": "Given a completed express delivery that exceeded the 2-hour window, the refund amount equals exactly the express fee, not the base delivery fee."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-6",
      "rule": "No refund is issued if the customer provided an incorrect delivery address, even if the delivery window was missed.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "Fault-attribution rule — the delivery window commitment is void when the failure is caused by customer error. Allowing refunds for customer-caused failures would create a moral hazard.",
      "testable_assertion": "Given a missed delivery window where the order is flagged as 'wrong address provided by customer', no express fee refund is issued."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-7",
      "rule": "If an express driver cancels mid-delivery and no replacement is assigned within 10 minutes, the express fee is automatically refunded.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "The 10-minute threshold is a policy decision that could be adjusted. The principle (auto-refund on failed reassignment) is strong, but the exact window is tunable.",
      "testable_assertion": "Given a driver cancellation on an express order, if 10 minutes elapse without a replacement driver being assigned, the system automatically initiates an express fee refund."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-8",
      "rule": "Express delivery refunds are processed within 3 business days.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "Processing timeline is an operational/financial policy, not a system integrity constraint. Could be shortened or lengthened based on payment processor capabilities.",
      "testable_assertion": "Given an approved express fee refund, the refund transaction is initiated no later than 3 business days after approval."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-9",
      "rule": "Customers with express orders must have access to real-time driver tracking.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "Product feature requirement, not a data integrity constraint. Important for UX but the system is not 'corrupt' without it.",
      "testable_assertion": "Given an express order with an assigned driver, the customer can view the driver's current location updated in near-real-time."
    }
  ],

  "acceptance_criteria": [],

  "edge_cases": [
    { "scenario": "Customer is exactly 10km from the nearest warehouse", "expected_behavior": "UNRESOLVED — is 10km inclusive or exclusive? Needs clarification. Recommend inclusive (<=10km) to avoid frustrating edge-case customers." },
    { "scenario": "Driver rating drops below 4 stars mid-delivery", "expected_behavior": "Current delivery should be completed (do not cancel in transit), but driver becomes ineligible for future express assignments." },
    { "scenario": "Driver cancels and replacement is assigned at minute 9 but replacement also cancels", "expected_behavior": "UNRESOLVED — does the 10-minute reassignment window reset? Recommend: it does NOT reset; original 10-minute clock from first cancellation governs the auto-refund." },
    { "scenario": "Customer changes address after order placement, new address is wrong", "expected_behavior": "UNRESOLVED — does 'wrong address' include post-placement address changes? Needs a clear definition of what constitutes a 'wrong address exception'." },
    { "scenario": "Order placed at 10:50pm, 2-hour window spans midnight", "expected_behavior": "UNRESOLVED — does the 2-hour window apply 24/7? Are there operating hours? No stakeholder mentioned time-of-day restrictions." },
    { "scenario": "Pickup SLA (15 min) is breached but delivery is still completed within 2 hours", "expected_behavior": "No refund triggered — the delivery window was met. Pickup SLA breach is an internal operational alert, not a customer-facing refund event." },
    { "scenario": "No express-qualified drivers available at time of order", "expected_behavior": "UNRESOLVED — should the system reject the express option (fail fast) or accept the order and hope a driver becomes available? Recommend: do not offer express if no qualified drivers are available within the warehouse radius." },
    { "scenario": "Customer disputes 'wrong address' determination", "expected_behavior": "UNRESOLVED — who adjudicates? No dispute resolution process defined. Likely needs a Customer Success manual review workflow." },
    { "scenario": "Multiple warehouses within 10km — which one fulfills?", "expected_behavior": "UNRESOLVED — no warehouse selection logic defined. Likely needs inventory availability and proximity-based routing." },
    { "scenario": "Refund already issued for missed window, then address found to be wrong after the fact", "expected_behavior": "UNRESOLVED — can a refund be clawed back? Finance and Legal need to weigh in on reversal policy." }
  ],

  "business_impact": {
    "financial": "Express fee ($12.99) creates a new revenue stream on eligible orders. Refund exposure exists for missed windows and failed reassignments. Wrong-address exception limits refund abuse. The 3-business-day refund processing window provides cash flow buffer. Key risk: if SLA miss rate is high, refund volume could erode the premium revenue entirely.",
    "operational": "15-minute pickup SLA and 4-star driver requirement constrain the available driver pool significantly. Express orders will compete with standard orders for high-rated drivers. The 10-minute reassignment window requires robust real-time dispatch capabilities. Real-time tracking adds infrastructure load.",
    "user": "Premium customers get faster delivery with real-time visibility, improving satisfaction for time-sensitive use cases. The 'guarantee' language (if used) sets high expectations — any miss damages trust disproportionately. The wrong-address refund exclusion could generate negative sentiment if not communicated clearly at checkout.",
    "risk": "The guarantee vs. best-effort contradiction is the highest business risk — marketing 'guaranteed 2-hour delivery' while Legal forbids contractual guarantees creates liability exposure and customer trust issues. Additionally, the driver pool constraint (4+ stars, within 15 min) may make express unavailable in practice in lower-density areas.",
    "stakeholders_affected": [
      { "group": "Customers", "impact": "New premium option with faster delivery and real-time tracking. Risk of disappointment if 'guarantee' language is used but windows are missed." },
      { "group": "Drivers", "impact": "High-rated drivers receive express assignments (potentially higher priority/volume). Sub-4-star drivers are excluded from express pool, creating a two-tier workforce." },
      { "group": "Warehouse Operations", "impact": "15-minute pickup SLA requires express orders to be prioritized in pick-and-pack workflows. May need dedicated express staging areas." },
      { "group": "Finance", "impact": "New revenue line from express fees. Refund processing within 3 business days adds workload. Need to monitor SLA miss rate vs. refund costs." },
      { "group": "Customer Success", "impact": "Will handle disputes around wrong-address exceptions and refund eligibility. Need clear escalation criteria and tooling." },
      { "group": "Legal/Compliance", "impact": "Must review all customer-facing language to ensure no contractual guarantee is implied. Refund must be framed as goodwill." }
    ]
  },

  "confidence": {
    "level": 38,
    "reasoning": "Confidence is low due to a fundamental contradiction between PM/Customer Success ('guaranteed') and Legal ('best effort only, not a guarantee'). This is not a minor terminology disagreement — it affects the core product promise, all customer-facing language, refund policy framing, and potential legal liability. Additionally, multiple critical edge cases are unresolved (driver availability, wrong-address definition, operating hours, warehouse selection). The spec is a raw workshop transcript, not a reviewed product brief.",
    "high_confidence_areas": [
      "Express fee amount and additive pricing structure",
      "10km warehouse proximity eligibility rule",
      "4-star minimum driver rating for express",
      "Express fee refund scope (express fee only, not base fee)",
      "Wrong-address refund exclusion exists as a concept"
    ],
    "low_confidence_areas": [
      "Whether the 2-hour delivery window is a guarantee or best-effort SLA",
      "Exact definition and adjudication of 'wrong address'",
      "Operating hours and time-of-day constraints",
      "Behavior when no express-qualified drivers are available",
      "Warehouse selection logic when multiple are in range",
      "Whether the auto-refund on failed reassignment conflicts with the 3-business-day processing timeline",
      "Dispute resolution process for refund exclusions"
    ],
    "assumptions": [
      "The 10km distance is calculated as straight-line (geodesic) distance, not driving distance — needs confirmation",
      "Driver rating is a rolling average, not per-delivery — but refresh frequency and calculation method are undefined",
      "The 2-hour window starts at order placement time, not at pickup time — PM said 'within 2 hours' without specifying the clock start",
      "Real-time tracking means GPS-based location updates at sub-minute intervals — no specific refresh rate defined",
      "The $12.99 express fee is flat, not distance-based or demand-based — no mention of surge pricing"
    ]
  },

  "escalations": [
    {
      "type": "contradiction",
      "description": "PM and Customer Success describe the 2-hour delivery window as a 'guarantee' shown prominently to customers. Legal explicitly states this cannot be a guarantee — it must be a 'best effort' SLA with refunds framed as 'goodwill gestures.' This contradiction affects every customer-facing touchpoint, the refund policy's legal standing, and the core product positioning. The product cannot simultaneously promise a guarantee and disclaim it.",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Customers", "Legal/Compliance", "Customer Success", "Marketing"],
      "options": [
        "A: Frame as 'Express Target' — communicate a 2-hour target with a goodwill refund policy, never using the word 'guarantee.' Aligns with Legal but weakens PM's product vision.",
        "B: Frame as 'Express Promise' with legal safe harbor — use 'guarantee' in marketing but include terms-of-service language limiting it to a goodwill refund. Common in industry but creates legal gray area.",
        "C: Escalate to executive sponsor to make a binding decision on product positioning and acceptable legal risk."
      ],
      "recommendation": "Option C — this is a foundational product decision that cannot be resolved at the workshop level. Executive sponsor must decide the company's risk appetite. In the interim, all implementation should use neutral language ('Express Delivery Window') and avoid 'guarantee' in any persisted data, enums, or API contracts."
    },
    {
      "type": "ambiguity",
      "description": "The relationship between automatic refunds (Customer Success: 'refund automatically' on failed reassignment) and the 3-business-day processing window (Finance) is unclear. Is the 'automatic' refund initiated immediately but processed within 3 days? Or does 'automatic' mean instant credit? These are different operational flows with different system and financial requirements.",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Finance", "Customer Success", "Customers"],
      "options": [
        "A: Auto-refund means immediate approval, processing still takes up to 3 business days.",
        "B: Auto-refund means instant credit to customer account/wallet, with settlement happening in backend within 3 days.",
        "C: Two separate refund paths — manual (3-day) for standard SLA misses, instant for driver cancellation scenarios."
      ],
      "recommendation": "Option A — auto-refund triggers immediate approval without human review, but financial processing follows the standard 3-business-day settlement. This satisfies Customer Success's intent (no manual intervention) while respecting Finance's processing reality."
    },
    {
      "type": "missing_context",
      "description": "No stakeholder addressed what happens when express-qualified drivers are unavailable at order time. Should the system hide the express option, show it as unavailable, accept the order optimistically, or queue it? This directly affects customer experience and SLA miss rates.",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Customers", "Operations", "Product"],
      "options": [
        "A: Hide express option entirely when no qualified drivers are available within the service radius.",
        "B: Show express as 'temporarily unavailable' with estimated availability time.",
        "C: Accept the order and attempt to find a driver, refunding if pickup SLA is breached."
      ],
      "recommendation": "Option A — do not offer what you cannot fulfill. Accepting orders without available drivers virtually guarantees SLA misses and refunds, destroying both customer trust and unit economics."
    },
    {
      "type": "missing_context",
      "description": "No definition of 'wrong address' was provided. This is the sole refund exclusion and will be contested by customers. Without a clear, auditable definition (typo in street number? Valid address but customer moved? Apartment number missing?), this rule is unenforceable and will create inconsistent Customer Success decisions.",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Customers", "Customer Success", "Legal/Compliance"],
      "options": [
        "A: Define 'wrong address' as any address where the driver arrives and cannot complete delivery due to address accuracy issues, verified by driver GPS + photo evidence.",
        "B: Define 'wrong address' narrowly as geocoding failure — the address does not resolve to a valid location.",
        "C: Defer to Customer Success case-by-case review with guidelines."
      ],
      "recommendation": "Option A with a dispute path — driver-verified with GPS and photo evidence provides an auditable trail. Customers must have a dispute mechanism reviewed within 24 hours."
    }
  ],

  "rejection_reasons": []
}
